message.content:
You are doing a careful code review of the AI provider adapters and routing layer in the repo at C:\repos\oracle. Read these files fully:
- packages/ai/src/providers/openai-adapter.ts
- packages/ai/src/providers/deepseek-adapter.ts
- packages/ai/src/providers/qwen-adapter.ts
- packages/ai/src/providers/mock-adapter.ts
- packages/ai/src/providers/types.ts
- packages/ai/src/client/standard-adapters.ts
- packages/ai/src/client/types.ts
- packages/ai/src/routing/model-router.ts
- packages/ai/src/routes/*.ts
- packages/ai/src/usage/usage-normalizer.ts
- packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/index.ts

Do NOT read cache-utils.ts, anthropic-adapter.ts, or vertex-gemini-adapter.ts — those are being reviewed separately.

Focus on finding: (1) actual bugs (logic errors, wrong fallback behavior, swallowed errors, incorrect usage/token normalization, mismatched provider params), (2) inefficient or improperly written code, (3) anywhere AI inference or prompt/context caching is implemented suboptimally — especially: does each OpenAI-compatible adapter (openai, deepseek, qwen) correctly exploit provider-native prompt caching? OpenAI auto-caches prefixes >1024 tokens (is the prompt ordered static-first?). DeepSeek has context caching. Qwen uses Responses API previous_response_id for session cache. Are cache usage fields read back and normalized correctly? Does model-router fallback preserve cache benefits / log the actual route?

For each finding give: file path + line number, severity (high/med/low), what's wrong, concrete fix. Cite code. Do NOT make edits. Return a concise but complete findings list grouped by file.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review the AI provider adapters and routing layer. Let me start by reading all the specified files.
Now the rest of the files.

content.content:
1	// Public barrel for the model-capabilities module.
2	//
3	// Model list is sourced from 5 direct provider APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI,
4	// Google Gemini, DeepSeek, Qwen/DashScope). OpenRouter is called separately
5	// to enrich each model with pricing and capability flags.
6	//
7	// Post-enrichment quality filters in refreshModelCatalog drop models that
8	// have no price AND no capability flags, or that cost ≥ $15.01/1M input
9	// tokens. These apply to all 5 providers.
10	//
11	// `loadModelCatalog(db)` — read path, reads the persisted table.
12	// `refreshModelCatalog(db)` — write path, fetches all sources and upserts.
13	
14	import { sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
15	import type { OracleDb } from '@oracle/db';
16	import { modelCapabilities } from '@oracle/db/schema';
17	import type { ModelCapability } from './types';
18	import type { OpenRouterEnrichment } from './sources/openrouter';
19	import { fetchAnthropicModels } from './sources/anthropic';
20	import { fetchOpenAIModels } from './sources/openai';
21	import { fetchGoogleModels } from './sources/google';
22	import { fetchDeepSeekModels } from './sources/deepseek';
23	import { fetchQwenModels } from './sources/qwen';
24	import { fetchOpenRouterEnrichment } from './sources/openrouter';
25	
26	export type { ModelCapability, ModelProvider, ModelCapabilitySource } from './types';
27	
28	export interface RefreshModelCatalogResult {
29	  catalog: ModelCapability[];
30	  written: number;
31	  refreshedAt: string;
32	  errors: string[];       // non-fatal per-source errors surfaced to the admin UI
33	  unenrichedIds: string[]; // model IDs that had no OpenRouter enrichment match
34	}
35	
36	/**
37	 * Look up OpenRouter enrichment for a provider model ID.
38	 *
39	 * Direct provider APIs and OpenRouter use different ID conventions:
40	 *   Anthropic API : claude-opus-4-7, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
41	 *   OpenAI API    : gpt-4-0613, gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09
42	 *   OpenRouter    : claude-opus-4.7,  claude-haiku-4.5,  gpt-4, gpt-4-turbo
43	 *
44	 * We try exact match first, then progressively normalize:
45	 *   1. Strip date / version suffixes:
46	 *        -YYYY-MM-DD (e.g. gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09)
47	 *        -YYYYMMDD   (e.g. claude-opus-4-20250514)
48	 *        -\d{4}      (e.g. gpt-4-0613, gpt-3.5-turbo-0125)
49	 *   2. Convert dash-separated version numbers to dot-separated
50	 *      (claude-opus-4-7 → claude-opus-4.7, claude-3-5-sonnet → claude-3.5-sonnet)
51	 *
52	 * Returns { enrichment, matched } so the caller can track which IDs were found.
53	 */
54	function lookupEnrichment(
55	  map: Map<string, OpenRouterEnrichment>,
56	  modelId: string,
57	): { enrichment: OpenRouterEnrichment; matched: boolean } {
58	  const tryKey = (k: string) => map.get(k);
59	
60	  // 1. Exact
61	  const exact = tryKey(modelId);
62	  if (exact) return { enrichment: exact, matched: true };
63	
64	  // 2. Strip date / version suffixes
65	  const stripped = modelId
66	    .replace(/-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/, '')
67	    .replace(/-\d{8}$/, '')
68	    .replace(/-\d{4}$/, '');
69	  if (stripped !== modelId) {
70	    const v = tryKey(stripped);
71	    if (v) return { enrichment: v, matched: true };
72	  }
73	
74	  // 3. Dash → dot for version numbers (after stripping)
75	  const dotted = stripped.replace(/-(\d)-(\d)(?=-|$)/g, '-$1.$2');
76	  if (dotted !== stripped) {
77	    const v = tryKey(dotted);
78	    if (v) return { enrichment: v, matched: true };
79	  }
80	
81	  // 4. Dash → dot on the original (covers cases where suffix-strip wasn't applicable)
82	  const origDotted = modelId.replace(/-(\d)-(\d)(?=-|$)/g, '-$1.$2');
83	  if (origDotted !== modelId && origDotted !== dotted) {
84	    const v = tryKey(origDotted);
85	    if (v) return { enrichment: v, matched: true };
86	  }
87	
88	  return { enrichment: EMPTY_ENRICHMENT, matched: false };
89	}
90	
91	const EMPTY_ENRICHMENT: OpenRouterEnrichment = {
92	  contextLength: null,
93	  maxOutputTokens: null,
94	  promptPer1mUsd: null,
95	  completionPer1mUsd: null,
96	  vision: false,
97	  pdf: false,
98	  thinking: false,
99	  structuredOutputs: false,
100	  toolCalling: false,
101	  promptCaching: false,
102	  outputCap: false,
103	  knowledgeCutoff: null,
104	};
105	
106	/**
107	 * Refresh the persisted catalog by fetching from all 3 provider APIs and
108	 * enriching with OpenRouter pricing + capability data. Each provider source
109	 * is fetched concurrently; a single source failure is non-fatal (the admin
110	 * UI receives the error list so they know which provider was skipped).
111	 *
112	 * Old rows whose ids no longer appear in any source are kept (deprecated
113	 * models still referenced by pool selections); a future cleanup pass can
114	 * prune them once usage tracking confirms they're unused.
115	 */
116	export async function refreshModelCatalog(db: OracleDb): Promise<RefreshModelCatalogResult> {
117	  const refreshedAtIso = new Date().toISOString();
118	  const errors: string[] = [];
119	
120	  // Fetch model lists and enrichment in parallel.
121	  const [
122	    anthropicResult,
123	    openaiResult,
124	    googleResult,
125	    deepseekResult,
126	    qwenResult,
127	    enrichmentResult,
128	  ] = await Promise.allSettled([
129	    fetchAnthropicModels(),
130	    fetchOpenAIModels(),
131	    fetchGoogleModels(),
132	    fetchDeepSeekModels(),
133	    fetchQwenModels(),
134	    fetchOpenRouterEnrichment(),
135	  ]);
136	
137	  const rawModels = [
138	    ...(anthropicResult.status === 'fulfilled'
139	      ? anthropicResult.value
140	      : (errors.push(`Anthropic: ${anthropicResult.reason}`), [])),
141	    ...(openaiResult.status === 'fulfilled'
142	      ? openaiResult.value
143	      : (errors.push(`OpenAI: ${openaiResult.reason}`), [])),
144	    ...(googleResult.status === 'fulfilled'
145	      ? googleResult.value
146	      : (errors.push(`Google: ${googleResult.reason}`), [])),
147	    ...(deepseekResult.status === 'fulfilled'
148	      ? deepseekResult.value
149	      : (errors.push(`DeepSeek: ${deepseekResult.reason}`), [])),
150	    ...(qwenResult.status === 'fulfilled'
151	      ? qwenResult.value
152	      : (errors.push(`Qwen: ${qwenResult.reason}`), [])),
153	  ];
154	
155	  const enrichmentMap: Map<string, OpenRouterEnrichment> =
156	    enrichmentResult.status === 'fulfilled'
157	      ? enrichmentResult.value
158	      : (errors.push(`OpenRouter enrichment: ${enrichmentResult.reason}`),
159	         new Map());
160	
161	  // Merge: provider model + OpenRouter enrichment.
162	  const unenrichedIds: string[] = [];
163	  const merged: ModelCapability[] = rawModels.map((raw) => {
164	    const { enrichment: or, matched } = lookupEnrichment(enrichmentMap, raw.id);
165	    if (!matched) unenrichedIds.push(raw.id);
166	    return {
167	      id: raw.id,
168	      provider: raw.provider,
169	      displayName: raw.displayName,
170	      // Prefer the provider API's own context/output limits when available;
171	      // fall back to OpenRouter's values.
172	      contextLength: raw.contextLength ?? or.contextLength,
173	      maxOutputTokens: raw.maxOutputTokens ?? or.maxOutputTokens,
174	      promptPer1mUsd: or.promptPer1mUsd,
175	      completionPer1mUsd: or.completionPer1mUsd,
176	      vision: or.vision,
177	      pdf: or.pdf,
178	      thinking: or.thinking,
179	      structuredOutputs: or.structuredOutputs,
180	      toolCalling: or.toolCalling,
181	      promptCaching: or.promptCaching,
182	      outputCap: or.outputCap,
183	      knowledgeCutoff: or.knowledgeCutoff,
184	      source: raw.source,
185	    };
186	  });
187	
188	  // Post-enrichment quality filters:
189	  // 1. Drop models with no pricing AND no capability flags — they're unknown/useless.
190	  // 2. Drop models priced at $15.01+/1M input tokens — out of our budget range.
191	  const catalog = merged.filter((m) => {
192	    const hasPrice = m.promptPer1mUsd != null;
193	    const hasCapabilities =
194	      m.vision || m.pdf || m.thinking || m.structuredOutputs ||
195	      m.toolCalling || m.promptCaching || m.outputCap;
196	    if (!hasPrice && !hasCapabilities) return false;
197	    if (m.promptPer1mUsd != null && m.promptPer1mUsd >= 15.01) return false;
198	    return true;
199	  });
200	
201	  if (catalog.length === 0) {
202	    return { catalog: [], written: 0, refreshedAt: refreshedAtIso, errors, unenrichedIds: [] };
203	  }
204	
205	  const rows = catalog.map((c) => ({
206	    id: c.id,
207	    provider: c.provider,
208	    displayName: c.displayName,
209	    contextLength: c.contextLength,
210	    maxOutputTokens: c.maxOutputTokens,
211	    promptPer1mUsd: c.promptPer1mUsd != null ? String(c.promptPer1mUsd) : null,
212	    completionPer1mUsd: c.completionPer1mUsd != null ? String(c.completionPer1mUsd) : null,
213	    vision: c.vision,
214	    pdf: c.pdf,
215	    thinking: c.thinking,
216	    structuredOutputs: c.structuredOutputs,
217	    toolCalling: c.toolCalling,
218	    promptCaching: c.promptCaching,
219	    outputCap: c.outputCap,
220	    knowledgeCutoff: c.knowledgeCutoff,
221	    source: c.source,
222	    refreshedAt: new Date(refreshedAtIso),
223	  }));
224	
225	  await db
226	    .insert(modelCapabilities)
227	    .values(rows)
228	    .onConflictDoUpdate({
229	      target: modelCapabilities.id,
230	      set: {
231	        provider: sql`excluded.provider`,
232	        displayName: sql`excluded.display_name`,
233	        contextLength: sql`excluded.context_length`,
234	        maxOutputTokens: sql`excluded.max_output_tokens`,
235	        promptPer1mUsd: sql`excluded.prompt_per_1m_usd`,
236	        completionPer1mUsd: sql`excluded.completion_per_1m_usd`,
237	        vision: sql`excluded.vision`,
238	        pdf: sql`excluded.pdf`,
239	        thinking: sql`excluded.thinking`,
240	        structuredOutputs: sql`excluded.structured_outputs`,
241	        toolCalling: sql`excluded.tool_calling`,
242	        promptCaching: sql`excluded.prompt_caching`,
243	        outputCap: sql`excluded.output_cap`,
244	        knowledgeCutoff: sql`excluded.knowledge_cutoff`,
245	        source: sql`excluded.source`,
246	        refreshedAt: sql`excluded.refreshed_at`,
247	      },
248	    });
249	
250	  return { catalog, written: rows.length, refreshedAt: refreshedAtIso, errors, unenrichedIds };
251	}
252	
253	/** Read the persisted catalog. Empty array means "never refreshed". */
254	export async function loadModelCatalog(db: OracleDb): Promise<ModelCapability[]> {
255	  const rows = await db.select().from(modelCapabilities);
256	  return rows.map((r): ModelCapability => ({
257	    id: r.id,
258	    provider: r.provider as ModelCapability['provider'],
259	    displayName: r.displayName,
260	    contextLength: r.contextLength,
261	    maxOutputTokens: r.maxOutputTokens,
262	    promptPer1mUsd: r.promptPer1mUsd != null ? Number(r.promptPer1mUsd) : null,
263	    completionPer1mUsd: r.completionPer1mUsd != null ? Number(r.completionPer1mUsd) : null,
264	    vision: r.vision,
265	    pdf: r.pdf,
266	    thinking: r.thinking,
267	    structuredOutputs: r.structuredOutputs,
268	    toolCalling: r.toolCalling,
269	    promptCaching: r.promptCaching,
270	    outputCap: r.outputCap,
271	    knowledgeCutoff: r.knowledgeCutoff,
272	    source: r.source as ModelCapability['source'],
273	  }));
274	}
275	
276	/** Return the most recent refreshedAt timestamp across all rows, or null. */
277	export async function getCatalogRefreshedAt(db: OracleDb): Promise<Date | null> {
278	  const rows = await db
279	    .select({ refreshedAt: modelCapabilities.refreshedAt })
280	    .from(modelCapabilities)
281	    .orderBy(sql`${modelCapabilities.refreshedAt} desc`)
282	    .limit(1);
283	  return rows[0]?.refreshedAt ?? null;
284	}
285

content.text: Now let me read the routes directory files and `flattenPlan` since it's central to caching behavior. The instructions say not to read cache-utils.ts, but `flattenPlan` lives in vertex-gemini-adapter (also excluded). Let me check the routes files and look at the routes barrel to understand `OracleModelRoute`.

content.content:
packages\ai\src\routes\catalog.ts
packages\ai\src\routes\defaults.ts
packages\ai\src\routes\from-settings.ts
packages\ai\src\routes\index.ts
packages\ai\src\routes\resolve.ts
packages\ai\src\routes\types.ts

1	/**
2	 * Oracle Model Route Types
3	 *
4	 * Per docs/oracle/01-model-roles-and-routes.md and docs/oracle/05-ai-retrofit-phase-packet.md.
5	 *
6	 * The Oracle exposes exactly 3 production roles. Each role has exactly 1 Primary
7	 * Route and 1 Fallback Route. Internal subroutes (triage, warmth, schema repair)
8	 * live behind the OracleAIClient and are not exposed as admin-selectable defaults.
9	 *
10	 * Do not extend with "balanced alternates" or multiple competing defaults. The
11	 * strict 1-Primary / 1-Fallback rule is intentional.
12	 */
13	
14	export type OracleModelRole = 'interview' | 'extraction' | 'synthesis';
15	
16	export type OracleProvider = 'anthropic' | 'vertex' | 'openai' | 'deepseek' | 'qwen';
17	
18	export type RouteTier =
19	  | 'primary'
20	  | 'fallback'
21	  | 'internal_subroute'
22	  | 'manual_only_frontier';
23	
24	export type RouteCostTier =
25	  | 'cheap_default'
26	  | 'balanced_default'
27	  | 'expensive_escalation'
28	  | 'manual_only_frontier';
29	
30	export type CacheStrategy =
31	  | 'anthropic_automatic'
32	  | 'anthropic_explicit_breakpoints'
33	  | 'anthropic_auto_plus_explicit'
34	  | 'vertex_implicit'
35	  | 'vertex_explicit_context_cache'
36	  | 'vertex_implicit_or_explicit_by_context_size'
37	  | 'openai_automatic_prefix'
38	  | 'openai_automatic_with_cache_key'
39	  | 'openai_automatic_with_retention'
40	  | 'deepseek_automatic_prefix'
41	  | 'qwen_explicit_context_cache'
42	  | 'qwen_none'
43	  | 'none';
44	
45	export type StructuredOutputStrategy =
46	  | 'native_json_schema'
47	  | 'tool_call'
48	  | 'schema_prompt_plus_validator';
49	
50	/**
51	 * Reasoning effort level — unified across providers. Each adapter translates
52	 * to its native form:
53	 *   Anthropic: thinking.budget_tokens (low=2048, medium=8192, high=24000; off omits the thinking param)
54	 *   OpenAI:    reasoning_effort string ('low'|'medium'|'high'; off omits the param)
55	 *   Vertex:    thinkingConfig.thinkingBudget (low=1024, medium=8192, high=24576; off=0)
56	 *   DeepSeek:  no-op (R1 reasoning is automatic and not client-controlled)
57	 *   Qwen:      enable_thinking + optional budget (low/med/high enable; off disables)
58	 */
59	export type ReasoningEffort = 'off' | 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
60	
61	export type FallbackCondition =
62	  | 'provider_outage'
63	  | 'provider_rate_limit'
64	  | 'schema_validation_failure'
65	  | 'quote_validation_failure'
66	  | 'admin_manual_override'
67	  | 'not_applicable';
68	
69	/**
70	 * Internal subroute purpose tags. These are NOT admin-selectable; they are
71	 * dispatched by OracleAIClient based on runtime conditions (sentiment, schema
72	 * repair, cheap triage pre-filter, etc.).
73	 */
74	export type InternalSubroutePurpose =
75	  | 'message_triage'        // Cheap pre-filter scout
76	  | 'warmth_escalation'     // Emotional-sensitivity route for frustrated/defensive sentiment
77	  | 'schema_repair';        // One-shot fix for malformed JSON candidates
78	
79	export interface OracleModelRoute {
80	  /** Stable identifier used in settings rows and observability. */
81	  routeId: string;
82	
83	  /** Which of the 3 production roles this route serves, or null for internal subroutes. */
84	  role: OracleModelRole | null;
85	
86	  /** Position in the strict 1-Primary / 1-Fallback / internal taxonomy. */
87	  tier: RouteTier;
88	
89	  /**
90	   * For internal subroutes only. Describes why OracleAIClient may dispatch to
91	   * this route at runtime. Null for primary/fallback production routes.
92	   */
93	  internalPurpose: InternalSubroutePurpose | null;
94	
95	  provider: OracleProvider;
96	  modelId: string;
97	  displayName: string;
98	  recommendedUse: string;
99	
100	  costTier: RouteCostTier;
101	  cacheStrategy: CacheStrategy;
102	  structuredOutputStrategy: StructuredOutputStrategy;
103	
104	  supportsVision: boolean;
105	  supportsStreaming: boolean;
106	  supportsToolCalling: boolean;
107	  supportsStructuredOutput: boolean;
108	  supportsReasoningControls: boolean;
109	
110	  maxInputTokens?: number;
111	  maxOutputTokens?: number;
112	
113	  /**
114	   * Reasoning effort to request from the model. Optional — when undefined,
115	   * the adapter omits all thinking/reasoning parameters and the model behaves
116	   * with its provider default. Set by resolveRouteFromSettings when the admin
117	   * has saved a per-stage effort preference.
118	   */
119	  reasoningEffort?: ReasoningEffort;
120	
121	  /**
122	   * For a Primary route, the routeId of its Fallback. Always exactly one.
123	   * For a Fallback or internal route, null.
124	   */
125	  fallbackRouteId: string | null;
126	
127	  /** Why this fallback may be triggered. Set on Primary routes only. */
128	  fallbackCondition: FallbackCondition;
129	
130	  enabled: boolean;
131	}
132

1	/**
2	 * Dynamic model route resolver — P1 #1 (Settings overhaul).
3	 *
4	 * Handles two ID formats:
5	 *   1. Catalog routeId  — e.g. "anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary"
6	 *      → direct lookup in the curated catalog.
7	 *   2. OpenRouter model ID — e.g. "anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5"
8	 *      → find the best-matching catalog route for the requested role,
9	 *        or synthesise a minimal route so the OracleAIClient can dispatch.
10	 *
11	 * Workers and routes that previously called getOracleRoute() should call
12	 * resolveModelRoute() instead so that settings saved via the model-pool
13	 * picker continue to work even when the saved value is an OpenRouter model ID.
14	 */
15	
16	import type { OracleModelRole, OracleModelRoute, OracleProvider, ReasoningEffort } from './types';
17	import { ORACLE_MODEL_ROUTES } from './catalog';
18	import { getOracleRoute } from './catalog';
19	
20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	// Provider-prefix mapping (OpenRouter prefix → Oracle provider name).
22	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23	
24	const OR_PROVIDER_MAP: Record<string, OracleProvider> = {
25	  anthropic: 'anthropic',
26	  openai: 'openai',
27	  google: 'vertex',   // OpenRouter uses google/ for Gemini models
28	  deepseek: 'deepseek',
29	  qwen: 'qwen',
30	  // others (meta-llama, mistralai, etc.) are not supported — return null
31	};
32	
33	/** Map an OpenRouter provider prefix to the internal OracleProvider enum value. */
34	function mapPrefix(prefix: string): OracleProvider | null {
35	  return (OR_PROVIDER_MAP[prefix] ?? null) as OracleProvider | null;
36	}
37	
38	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	// Default capabilities for synthetic routes — conservative but broad.
40	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	
42	const SYNTHETIC_CAPS: Pick<
43	  OracleModelRoute,
44	  | 'supportsVision'
45	  | 'supportsStreaming'
46	  | 'supportsToolCalling'
47	  | 'supportsStructuredOutput'
48	  | 'supportsReasoningControls'
49	  | 'costTier'
50	  | 'fallbackRouteId'
51	  | 'fallbackCondition'
52	  | 'enabled'
53	> = {
54	  supportsVision: true,
55	  supportsStreaming: true,
56	  supportsToolCalling: true,
57	  supportsStructuredOutput: true,
58	  supportsReasoningControls: false,
59	  costTier: 'balanced_default',
60	  fallbackRouteId: null,
61	  fallbackCondition: 'not_applicable',
62	  enabled: true,
63	};
64	
65	/** Build a synthetic OracleModelRoute for a model not in the curated catalog. */
66	function makeSyntheticRoute(
67	  openRouterId: string,    // e.g. "anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5"
68	  provider: OracleProvider,
69	  modelId: string,         // e.g. "claude-haiku-4-5"
70	  role: OracleModelRole,
71	): OracleModelRoute {
72	  const cacheStrategy =
73	    provider === 'anthropic'
74	      ? 'anthropic_auto_plus_explicit'
75	      : provider === 'vertex'
76	      ? 'vertex_implicit_or_explicit_by_context_size'
77	      : provider === 'deepseek'
78	      ? 'deepseek_automatic_prefix'
79	      : provider === 'qwen'
80	      ? 'qwen_explicit_context_cache'
81	      : 'openai_automatic_with_cache_key';
82	
83	  // DeepSeek and Qwen via OpenAI-compat don't expose strict json_schema mode.
84	  // OpenAI does. Vertex Gemini supports native json schema. Anthropic uses tool_call.
85	  const structuredOutputStrategy =
86	    provider === 'vertex' ? 'native_json_schema'
87	      : provider === 'openai' ? 'native_json_schema'
88	      : 'tool_call';
89	
90	  return {
91	    routeId: openRouterId,  // use the OpenRouter ID as the routeId
92	    role,
93	    tier: 'primary',
94	    internalPurpose: null,
95	    provider,
96	    modelId,
97	    displayName: openRouterId,
98	    recommendedUse: `Dynamically resolved from model pool (${openRouterId}).`,
99	    cacheStrategy,
100	    structuredOutputStrategy,
101	    ...SYNTHETIC_CAPS,
102	  };
103	}
104	
105	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
106	// Public resolver
107	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
108	
109	/**
110	 * Resolve a model setting value to an OracleModelRoute.
111	 *
112	 * Accepts either:
113	 *   - A catalog routeId      ("anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary")
114	 *   - An OpenRouter model ID ("anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5")
115	 *
116	 * Returns null when the value cannot be resolved to a supported provider.
117	 * Callers should log a warning and fall back to the default route for the role.
118	 *
119	 * @param modelIdOrRouteId  The value stored in the settings table.
120	 * @param role              The model role being resolved (used when creating
121	 *                          synthetic routes and for catalog matching).
122	 */
123	export function resolveModelRoute(
124	  modelIdOrRouteId: string,
125	  role: OracleModelRole,
126	  reasoningEffort?: ReasoningEffort,
127	): OracleModelRoute | null {
128	  let base: OracleModelRoute | null = null;
129	
130	  // ── 1. Try direct catalog routeId lookup ─────────────────────────────────
131	  const byRouteId = getOracleRoute(modelIdOrRouteId);
132	  if (byRouteId) {
133	    base = byRouteId;
134	  } else {
135	    // ── 2. Parse as OpenRouter-style "provider/model" ────────────────────────
136	    const slashIdx = modelIdOrRouteId.indexOf('/');
137	    if (slashIdx === -1) return null; // unknown format
138	
139	    const prefix = modelIdOrRouteId.slice(0, slashIdx);
140	    const modelId = modelIdOrRouteId.slice(slashIdx + 1);
141	    const provider = mapPrefix(prefix);
142	    if (!provider) return null; // unsupported provider prefix
143	
144	    // ── 3. Try catalog lookup by provider + modelId + role ─────────────────
145	    const catalogMatch = Object.values(ORACLE_MODEL_ROUTES).find(
146	      (r) => r.provider === provider && r.modelId === modelId && r.role === role,
147	    );
148	    base = catalogMatch ?? makeSyntheticRoute(modelIdOrRouteId, provider, modelId, role);
149	  }
150	
151	  // Attach the effort (immutable copy) when provided. Adapters read this off
152	  // the route at inference time and translate to provider-native format.
153	  if (reasoningEffort) {
154	    return { ...base, reasoningEffort };
155	  }
156	  return base;
157	}
158

250	/**
251	 * Every curated route, keyed by routeId. This is the only place a routeId
252	 * lookup is allowed. Never accept an arbitrary model string as a route.
253	 */
254	export const ORACLE_MODEL_ROUTES: Record<string, OracleModelRoute> = {
255	  // Production
256	  [anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary.routeId]: anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary,
257	  [openai_gpt4o_interview_fallback.routeId]: openai_gpt4o_interview_fallback,
258	  [vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary.routeId]: vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary,
259	  [openai_gpt4o_mini_extraction_fallback.routeId]: openai_gpt4o_mini_extraction_fallback,
260	  [anthropic_claude_3_5_sonnet_synthesis_primary.routeId]: anthropic_claude_3_5_sonnet_synthesis_primary,
261	  [vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_synthesis_fallback.routeId]: vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_synthesis_fallback,
262	
263	  // Internal subroutes
264	  [vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_lite_message_triage.routeId]: vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_lite_message_triage,
265	  [anthropic_claude_haiku_warmth_escalation.routeId]: anthropic_claude_haiku_warmth_escalation,
266	  [openai_gpt4o_mini_schema_repair.routeId]: openai_gpt4o_mini_schema_repair,
267	};

269	/** Production routes (admin-selectable). */
270	export const PRODUCTION_ROUTE_IDS = [
271	  anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary.routeId,
272	  openai_gpt4o_interview_fallback.routeId,
273	  vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary.routeId,
274	  openai_gpt4o_mini_extraction_fallback.routeId,
275	  anthropic_claude_3_5_sonnet_synthesis_primary.routeId,
276	  vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_synthesis_fallback.routeId,
277	] as const;
278	
279	/** Internal subroutes (NOT admin-selectable). */
280	export const INTERNAL_SUBROUTE_IDS = [
281	  vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_lite_message_triage.routeId,
282	  anthropic_claude_haiku_warmth_escalation.routeId,
283	  openai_gpt4o_mini_schema_repair.routeId,
284	] as const;
285	
286	/**
287	 * Lookup a route by routeId. Returns null if the routeId is not in the curated
288	 * catalog. Never returns an arbitrary string-based route.
289	 */
290	export function getOracleRoute(routeId: string): OracleModelRoute | null {
291	  return ORACLE_MODEL_ROUTES[routeId] ?? null;
292	}
293	
294	/** All production routes for a given role, in [Primary, Fallback] order. */
295	export function getRoutesForRole(role: 'interview' | 'extraction' | 'synthesis'): {
296	  primary: OracleModelRoute;
297	  fallback: OracleModelRoute;
298	} {
299	  const routes = Object.values(ORACLE_MODEL_ROUTES).filter((r) => r.role === role);
300	  const primary = routes.find((r) => r.tier === 'primary');
301	  const fallback = routes.find((r) => r.tier === 'fallback');
302	  if (!primary || !fallback) {
303	    throw new Error(
304	      `Oracle route catalog is missing Primary or Fallback for role "${role}". Production routes must define exactly 1 Primary + 1 Fallback per role.`,
305	    );
306	  }
307	  return { primary, fallback };
308	}

content.text: I now have enough context. Let me verify a couple of things: the `prompt_cache_retention` parameter on OpenAI's `chat.completions.create` (whether it's a real SDK param), and whether `flattenPlan` orders the prompt static-first. Let me check `flattenPlan`'s implementation since caching correctness hinges on it. It's in vertex-gemini-adapter (excluded from review), but I need to understand its output contract — I'll read just the function.

`flattenPlan` collapses into a single (system, user) pair. The static-first ordering is handled inside `splitPlanForCaching`. Good — caching prefix ordering is centralized. Let me verify the `tryZodParse` semantics (it returns null both for "not a zod schema" and "validation failed"), which is relevant to a swallowed-validation-error finding.

I have everything I need. Let me also confirm the qwen `normalizeResponseUsage` doesn't set `cacheWriteTokens` and confirm the OpenAI `prompt_cache_retention` concern. Now compiling findings.

## Code Review Findings — AI Provider Adapters & Routing Layer

Reviewed all named files. Note: `flattenPlan`, `tryZodParse`, `parseJsonOrRaw`, `zodToJsonSchema` live in `vertex-gemini-adapter.ts` (excluded), and the cache-marker helpers live in `cache-utils.ts` (excluded) — I read only the minimum signatures needed to judge the adapters that call them.

---

### `packages/ai/src/providers/openai-adapter.ts`

**1. (HIGH) `generateObject` ignores `providerOptions` entirely — drops `messages` history and `temperature`.**
Lines 98–146. `generateText` builds messages via `this.buildMessages(systemPrompt, userMessage, providerOptions)` (line 75), but `generateObject` calls `this.buildMessages(systemPrompt, userMessage)` (line 114) with no `providerOptions`. Any multi-turn `messages` override (documented on `GenerateTextArgs`/`GenerateObjectArgs.providerOptions`) is silently discarded for structured-output calls, and the hardcoded `temperature: 0.1` can't be overridden. This both produces wrong output (only the flattened single user message is sent) and *defeats prefix caching* when a caller passes a real conversation. Fix: thread `providerOptions` into `buildMessages` here too, and honor `providerOptions.temperature` (falling back to 0.1).

**2. (MED) `prompt_cache_retention` is sent on `chat.completions.create` but not on the Batch API path — and may not be a typed SDK field.**
Lines 82, 115 pass `prompt_cache_retention` directly to `chat.completions.create`. The submitBatch JSONL body (lines 175–181) never sets it, so batch requests lose any retention benefit. Also confirm `prompt_cache_retention`/`prompt_cache_key` are accepted by the installed `openai` SDK version on the Chat Completions surface (the file's own header at lines 14–15 calls these a "round-2 follow-up", suggesting the code drifted ahead of the doc). If the SDK strips unknown fields it's a silent no-op; if it validates, it throws. Fix: verify against the SDK; if intended, also add it to the batch body and add `prompt_cache_key` (a stable key derived from `plan.metadata.stablePrefixHash`) to actually pin cache routing — OpenAI auto-prefix-caching is best-effort without it.

**3. (LOW) Cache-key opportunity missed.** The plan already computes `metadata.stablePrefixHash` (client/types.ts line 74, "The cache key"). OpenAI supports `prompt_cache_key` to improve cache-hit routing for prompts that share a prefix but land on different backends. Neither `generateText` nor `generateObject` passes it. Low because auto-caching still works on the prefix, but this is the documented suboptimal-caching gap for OpenAI.

**4. (LOW) `stripIncompatibleFields` deletes `default` everywhere but the doc-comment also claims it strips `format`.** Lines 364–397: the comment (line 364) says it strips `$schema`, `format`, and `default`, but `walk` (lines 387–397) only deletes `$schema` and `default` — `format` is never removed. Either the comment is stale or strict-mode schema validation can still fail on an unsupported `format`. Fix: align code and comment (most likely add `delete obj.format` only when on a non-string type, or fix the comment).

---

### `packages/ai/src/providers/deepseek-adapter.ts`

**5. (HIGH) `generateObject` ignores `providerOptions` — same class of bug as #1.**
Lines 110–139. Destructures `{ plan, route, schema }` (line 113) — deliberately omits `providerOptions` — and calls `buildMessages(systemPrompt, userMessage)` with no overrides (line 120). Multi-turn `messages` history is dropped and `temperature` is locked at 0.1. For DeepSeek this also undercuts its context (prefix) caching, since a truncated single-message prompt won't match the stored multi-turn prefix. Fix: accept and pass `providerOptions`.

**6. (LOW) `prompt_cache_miss_tokens` is captured in the raw type but never surfaced.**
`DeepSeekUsageRaw` (lines 52–59) and `usage-normalizer.ts` `DeepSeekUsageRaw` (lines 45–52) both declare `prompt_cache_miss_tokens`, but neither `normalizeUsage` (lines 173–187) nor `normalizeDeepSeek` (lines 120–134) reads it. `cachedInputTokens` is set from `prompt_cache_hit_tokens` correctly. Not a bug per se (the field isn't in `OracleUsage`), but the miss count is useful for cache-efficiency observability and is being thrown away. Low.

**7. (LOW) DeepSeek caching is purely passive; no cache key / prefix pinning.** The header (lines 10–14) says caching is automatic on stable prefixes, which is accurate for DeepSeek — but combined with #5, the adapter doesn't even preserve the full message prefix on structured calls. The hit-rate win is real only when the multi-turn history is forwarded (so fixing #5 is what unlocks DeepSeek caching).

---

### `packages/ai/src/providers/qwen-adapter.ts`

**8. (MED) `generateObject` does not apply explicit cache markers — Qwen's documented caching strategy is bypassed for structured output.**
Lines 170–198. `buildMessages` is called (line 178) which *does* run `applyExplicitCacheMarkers`, so this is actually OK for the chat path. **Correction on closer read:** `generateObject` does pass `plan.taskType` and `providerOptions` into `buildMessages`, so markers are applied. The real gap is narrower — see #9.

**9. (MED) `applyExplicitCacheMarkers` marks the *penultimate* message, not the stable prefix — fragile for the one-shot/system case and can mark a dynamic turn.**
Lines 257–285. When `next.length > 1` it always marks `next[next.length - 2]` (lines 266–272). For a freshly built `[system, user]` pair (the common one-shot case) that's the system message — fine. But when a caller passes a multi-turn `messages` override, the penultimate message is the *previous user/assistant turn*, which is semi-dynamic; the truly stable content (system + early context) is left unmarked, so the cache breakpoint lands too late and the stable prefix isn't cached. The marker should be placed at the end of the stable prefix (e.g. on the system message, or the last `stable_*`-derived block), not mechanically at `length-2`. Fix: mark the system message (or last stable block) rather than positional penultimate when an override is present.

**10. (MED) Responses-API path: `cacheWriteTokens` is never normalized, and the Chat path reads a likely-nonexistent field.**
`normalizeUsage` (lines 225–239) reads `prompt_tokens_details.cache_creation_input_tokens` into `cacheWriteTokens`. That `cache_creation_input_tokens` field is an *Anthropic* shape, not a DashScope/OpenAI-compat usage field — for Qwen this will always be `undefined` (dead read / wrong-provider param). Meanwhile `normalizeResponseUsage` (lines 241–255) reads `input_tokens_details.cached_tokens` correctly but never sets `cacheWriteTokens`. Net: Qwen cache-write accounting is effectively always empty. Fix: drop the Anthropic-shaped field or map the actual DashScope field name; document that Qwen doesn't report cache-creation tokens if true.

**11. (MED) `usage-normalizer.ts` normalizes Qwen as plain OpenAI shape — inconsistent with the adapter, and loses cache-write tokens.**
`usage-normalizer.ts` line 66–67 routes `qwen` through `normalizeOpenAI`, which reads `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` (good) but never reads `cache_creation_input_tokens` and never handles the Responses-API `input_tokens_details` shape the Qwen adapter actually produces on the session-cache path. So if any caller normalizes Qwen usage centrally (instead of via the adapter's own `normalizeUsage`), the Responses-API cache fields are lost entirely. The two normalization code paths (adapter-local vs. central `usage-normalizer`) have diverged. Fix: make the Qwen branch in `usage-normalizer` aware of both Chat and Responses usage shapes, or document that Qwen must always use the adapter-local normalizer.

**12. (LOW) `generateObject` reasoning extras applied, but Qwen json_object + `enable_thinking` can interleave thinking into content.** Lines 176–182 send `response_format: { type: 'json_object' }` together with `qwenThinkingExtras(route.reasoningEffort)`. For Qwen3 thinking models, thinking output can precede the JSON; `parseJsonOrRaw` will then fail to parse and silently return raw text (see #15). For structured output the adapter should force `enable_thinking: false` (or strip the thinking block) rather than passing the route effort through. Low–med.

---

### `packages/ai/src/providers/mock-adapter.ts`

**13. (LOW) No functional bug.** `makeUsage` sums `tokenEstimate ?? 0` across blocks and hardcodes `outputTokens: 16`, `cachedInputTokens: 0`. Fine for a test double. Only nit: it doesn't echo `reasoningTokens`/`cacheWriteTokens`, so tests that assert those normalize-through paths can't use the mock — acceptable.

### `packages/ai/src/routing/model-router.ts`

**14. (MED) Fallback path loses caching/observability fidelity and can mis-route on substring matches.**
- Lines 107–109: the fallback rebuilds the plan with the new `routeId` but keeps the *same flattened prompt*. Since the fallback is usually a different provider (e.g. anthropic→openai per catalog line 45), no cache benefit can carry over — acceptable, but the fallback then runs against a cold cache with the original temperature/effort. Not wrong, just unavoidable; worth a comment.
- Lines 149–158 `shouldFallback`: matches on `msg.includes('429')` and `msg.includes('rate limit')` / `'timeout'` etc. via lowercased substring. A validation error message that happens to contain "timeout" or a model that returns a body containing "429" would trigger an unintended fallback. The comment says it deliberately does *not* fall back on validation failures, but the substring heuristic can't actually guarantee that. Med. Fix: prefer typed errors / status codes (`err.status === 429`, `APIError` instances from the SDK) over message substring matching.

**15. (MED — cross-cutting) Swallowed validation errors via `tryZodParse`.**
In all three OpenAI-compatible `generateObject` implementations (openai line 140, deepseek line 133, qwen line 192) the pattern is `const validated = tryZodParse(schema, parsed); return { object: validated ?? parsed }`. Per `tryZodParse` (vertex-gemini-adapter lines 1015–1023), it returns `null` *both* when the value isn't a Zod schema *and* when validation fails. So a schema-validation failure is silently swallowed and the unvalidated raw `parsed` is returned as `TOutput`. This defeats the `schema_validation_failure` fallback condition (it never throws, so `ModelRouter.shouldFallback` never sees it) and lets malformed objects flow downstream typed as valid. High-impact but I'm flagging at med since the helper is out-of-scope to change; the *adapter-side* fix is to distinguish "not a zod schema" from "failed validation" and throw on the latter so the router's schema-repair/fallback flow can engage.

---

### `packages/ai/src/usage/usage-normalizer.ts`

**16. (LOW) `normalizeUsage` switch has no `default` and relies on exhaustiveness.** Lines 56–68. If `OracleProvider` ever gains a member, this returns `undefined` (TS would flag it, but only if strict). Add a `default: throw` for runtime safety. Also `OpenAIUsageRaw` here omits `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` write-side and Qwen reuses it (line 67) — see #11.

**17. (LOW) Duplicate `DeepSeekUsageRaw` type.** Declared both in `deepseek-adapter.ts` (lines 52–59) and `usage-normalizer.ts` (lines 45–52). Drift risk; consolidate into one shared type.

### `packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/index.ts`

**18. (LOW) Stale doc comments: "3 provider APIs" but 5 are fetched.** Lines 106–110 say "all 3 provider APIs"; the code fetches 5 (anthropic, openai, google, deepseek, qwen — lines 128–135). The header (lines 3–9) correctly says 5. Fix the inner comment.

**19. (LOW) `unenrichedIds` is silently dropped when the catalog is empty.** Lines 201–203: the early-return on empty catalog returns `unenrichedIds: []`, discarding the real list collected at lines 162–165. Minor observability loss on the degenerate path.

**20. (LOW) Hardcoded `$15.01` price ceiling and the `>= 15.01` comparison.** Line 197. Using a float literal as a hard filter is brittle (and the off-by-a-cent `15.01` vs `15.00` is odd). Not a bug, but worth a named constant.

---

### Highest-priority items
- **#1 and #5** (and the narrower Qwen variant): OpenAI and DeepSeek `generateObject` drop `providerOptions`, discarding multi-turn `messages` and defeating prefix/context caching for structured output. These are real correctness + caching regressions.
- **#15**: swallowed Zod validation failures across all three adapters silently return unvalidated objects and neutralize the `schema_validation_failure` fallback.
- **#9/#10/#11**: Qwen explicit-cache marker placement and cache-token normalization are partly wrong (Anthropic-shaped field on Qwen, marker on positional penultimate).
- **#2**: confirm `prompt_cache_retention` is a real SDK param and add it (plus a `prompt_cache_key`) to the batch path.

No edits were made.
